title: Closed-sketch-loop region detection (region/hole counts + area)
source: OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_loop_detector.cpp:11-30 — Case 1: rectangle 10x5 (4 lines) => success, faces.size()==1, faces[0].innerLoops EMPTY (no holes).
OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_loop_detector.cpp:32-61 — Case 2: outer 10x10 square + inner 4x4 square => success, faces.size()==1, faces[0].innerLoops.size()==1 (one hole).

OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_loop_detector.cpp:63-78 — Case 3: semicircle arc (center 0,0 r5 sweep 0..pi) + diameter line (5,0)->(-5,0) => success, faces NOT empty (>=1 region closed by arc+chord).

OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_loop_detector.cpp:80-94 — Case 4: single ellipse (center 0,0, rx6 ry3, rot 0.25) with planarizeIntersections => success, faces.size()==1, faces[0].outerLoop.area() > 50.0 (pi*6*3 ~= 56.5).

OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_face_builder.cpp:33-152 — FaceBuilder on the same loops: rectangle => 1 valid face; square+hole => 1 face with 2 WIRES (outer+hole, countWires==2); ellipse => 1 face, 1 wire. (Corroborates region->OCCT face build.)
OneCAD-CPP/tests/prototypes/proto_face_builder.cpp:92-131 — sketch-XY basis check: a 10x5 region built on SketchPlane XY lands at world x∈[-5,0], y∈[0,10], z==0 (confirms the non-standard XY basis).

arcAndChord.note: faces not empty; exact count not asserted

notes: Region/hole counts feed extrude/revolve profile selection and preview fill (SCHEMA §7.4 SketchRegions -> regions[] with outerLoop + holes[]). The square-with-hole case is the key multi-loop fixture: ONE region, ONE hole => an extrude of it produces a body with a through/blind pocket. The ellipse area is asserted only as '>50' (not an exact value) — recorded as such (approx 56.5 shown for orientation, not as an oracle). Loop detection uses makeRegionDetectionConfig() in the regen path (proto_regeneration Test 1).
